race.name: Gatineau Loppet
race.tagline: Fifty-one kilometers through Canada's capital region — Gatineau Park's frozen forests deliver North America's premier Worldloppet marathon.

vitals.aid_stations: 5 stations with warm drinks, maple syrup treats, and energy food

climate.description: February in the Ottawa-Gatineau region brings reliable cold and snow. The Canadian Shield terrain and northern latitude ensure consistent winter conditions. Temperatures are typically well below freezing, with occasional Arctic air masses pushing conditions to extreme cold.
challenges: Extreme cold when Arctic air masses descend — wind chill can reach -35°C
Hilly terrain in Gatineau Park exposes skiers to wind on ridgelines
Variable snow conditions between sheltered forest and open ridges
Cold-weather gear management critical for the 3-5 hour effort

course.primary: Hilly course through Gatineau Park, a 361 km² wilderness area across the river from Ottawa. The route traverses the Gatineau Hills with significant climbing through mixed hardwood and boreal forest. Rolling terrain with punchy climbs and fast descents demands strong technique.
course.surface: Machine-groomed trails through Gatineau Park's extensive trail network
course.grooming: Excellent — Gatineau Park maintains one of North America's best groomed trail systems
features: Course through Gatineau Park, a massive protected wilderness area
Hilly terrain with challenging climbs and technical descents
Views across the Ottawa River valley to Parliament Hill
Part of the Winterlude festival season in Canada's capital region

nordic_lab_rating.score_note: Top marks for grooming and accessibility — Gatineau Park is minutes from Ottawa International Airport. The hilly course earns a high technicality score. Strong bilingual community atmosphere. International draw limited by North American location relative to European Worldloppet circuit.
nordic_lab_rating.tier_note: Prestige 4 promotes one tier: raw T2 (71) stays TIER 2 (already T2)

history.summary: The Gatineau Loppet was founded in 1979 and quickly became Canada's premier marathon cross-country ski race. Set in Gatineau Park — a vast wilderness preserve just across the Ottawa River from Canada's Parliament — the race benefits from world-class trail infrastructure and a passionate bilingual skiing community. It joined the Worldloppet series and has been North America's most consistent representative on the circuit, drawing skiers from across Canada and the northeastern United States.
notable_facts: Gatineau Park covers 361 km² and maintains over 200km of groomed XC trails
The park is managed by the National Capital Commission, ensuring top-tier maintenance
Located just 15 minutes from downtown Ottawa, Canada's capital
Canadian XC skiing legends Pierre Harvey and Beckie Scott trained on these trails
The Gatineau Hills are part of the Canadian Shield, one of Earth's oldest geological formations
Bilingual French-English event reflects Canada's dual linguistic heritage

youtube_data.search_query: Gatineau Loppet cross country ski race Gatineau Park

videos.title: 42nd Annual Gatineau Loppet
videos.description:
Race reporter ActiveSteve was back on the snow for another weekend of fun, this time in Gatineau Park for the 42nd annual Gatineau Loppet. Part of the World Loppet series of races, this event attracted skiers from 16 different countries in distancs ranging from 5km to 50km in either the Classic Syle or Freestyle (Skate) Style of nordic skiing. Watch this video to get a sense of the trails and the organization of this world-class event in the heart of the Nations Capital!

Music: Audionautix.com

videos.title: 2013 Gatineau Loppet 55k Classic Race Review
videos.description:
ActiveSteve, not even recovered from tackling the Canadian Ski Marathon the week before, heads back out with his skis on to cover another classic Canadian ski event, the Gatineau Loppet, heading into it's 35th year. To commemorate the 35th year, the Classic event featured a 55km and 38km linear (point-to-point) route instead of the normal looped course. Under brilliant skies and great snow conditions, he lined up with participants from around the world. Check out the video to see how is all unrolled.

Music: Dan-O Songs

videos.title: 34th Annual Gatineau Loppet Race Weekend Review
videos.description:
Video race reporter ActiveSteve heads back out on skis to cover the 34th annual Gatineau Loppet. This race is part of the 15-event World Loppet series, and attracts racers from over 20 countries. There were events all weekend, and ActiveSteve took part in the 31km Skate Ski event, but hung around all weekend to see how the other races turned out. It was another steller day in the snow, and it looks like this is another race to put into your winter calendars!

Music: Kevin MacLeod
videos.transcript:

Once finished, racers made their way to

videos.title: Gatineau Loppet 2015
videos.description: Experience the excitement of Canada's World Loppet Cross-country Ski Race. This was my 25 and 26 time experiencing this challenging 2 day marathon on skis. It take place in February in the scenic Gatineau Hills just north of Canada's capital Ottawa.--Saul

videos.title: 2017 Gatineau Loppet Re-Cap
videos.description:
Join ActiveSteve as he spends another weekend enjoying the best that winter has to offer in the National Capital Region. This time, he takes part in back-to-back ski races as part of the Gatineau Loppet, starting off with a 51km classic ski race on Saturday, and finishing with a 27km skate ski race on Sunday. Watch this video to see what makes this such a special event, and make sure you add it to your winter calendar next year!

Music: TripleScoop Music

videos.title: Get on your skis! #5 - The Virtual Gatineau Loppet
videos.description:
The Gatineau Loppet is proud to present a series of informative videos to guide novice cross-country skiers. In this video, Louise and Olivier propose you a cross-country skiing challenge. It's the Virtual Gatineau Loppet which will take place from February 13th to 28th! All information on www.gatineauloppet.com.

Version française de la vidéo : https://youtu.be/cYu_AdTD16Q

About the animators :
Louise Poirier is a physical educator. She obtained a Master's degree in Human Kinetics at Université Laval. Originally an athlete and cross-country ski coach, Louise has more than 45 years of experience in this beautiful outdoor sport. For many years, she hosted the program "Bouffée de santé" on Radio-Canada. It is by completing 10 of the greatest cross-country ski races in the world that she obtained her title of "Worldloppet Master". She is proud to be the president of the Gatineau Loppet, the greatest cross-country ski race in Canada with a fantastic team of volunteers. 

Olivier Babineau is a kinesiologist. He has a Master's degree in Exercise Physiology from McGill University. An elite multi-sport athlete and new father, he aims to develop the vitality of people of all levels within the Fitness for Freedom company. Olivier converted to endurance sports following his many years in the world of high level soccer. He has represented Canada on several occasions in various sports including soccer, winter triathlon and trail running. After falling in love with cross-country skiing several years ago, he won the top 10 at the 50 km Gatineau Loppet. He secretly dreams of one day making a podium there and hopes to be able to discover the other international races of the "Worldloppet" series.

videos.title: What to do in Ottawa when skiing the Gatineau Loppet?
videos.description:
🇨🇦 Have you ever seen Ottawa? 
A day before I skied the Gatineau Loppet I took the chance to check out Canada’s capital! Local tourism guide Vladimir showed me around!

quotes.text: The tracks are delicious that's been dumping like 30 40 centimetres of snow freshly groomed and behind me you can see the hurricane that destroyed quite a lot last year.
Although it was cold the ski was pretty fast and I had a good time skiing through all the trails in fact it was so cold the batteries died and it got very little footage.
The day's temperature started out somewhere around minus 8 with a bright sun and almost no wind we had great Glide and kick to start things off making our way along the course.
